author: dblenkus
text:
Hi,

I am using `elasticsearch-dsl` in parallel Django tests. That means that each thread must have unique index name to prevent collisions. Till Django version 1.10 this hack worked:  
<https://github.com/genialis/resolwe/blob/10b6021908a97ab8fe8c1d82ce993758544408a8/resolwe/elastic/indices.py#L53>

But in Django 1.11 url configuration is imported earlier than before (still in the thread of test runner) which causes that only one instance of `DocType` object is initialized so all threads use the same index again.

I've tried overriding `DocType._get_index` method, but with no luck:

```
def _get_index(self, index=None):
    index = super(BaseDocument, self)._get_index(index)

    if getattr(settings, 'ELASTICSEARCH_TESTING', False):
        index = '{}_test_{}'.format(index, threading.get_ident())

    return index
```

Is there a recommended way of changing index name on the fly?

Thanks,  
Domen

author: WisdomPill
text: You can specify the index on every request by passing the index parameter in a Search() for example or a save(), init() etc... I hope it helps

author: dblenkus
text: Thanks! It turned out that at some places `DocType._doc_type.index` is accessed directly instead of using `DocType._get_index`. So the best solution, that I came up with, is to change index name directly in `DocType._doc_type.index`. But this has to be done before initializing `DocType` as otherwise it has no effect.
